issuesFound.title: Secondary text colour contrast insufficient against white background
issuesFound.explanation: The custom property `--colour-text-secondary` is defined as `#aaaaaa`, which against a white background (`--colour-neutral-0: #ffffff`) yields a contrast ratio of approximately 2.3:1. This fails WCAG 1.4.3 Contrast (Minimum) Level AA for normal text (requires 4.5:1), making content such as blockquotes and section descriptions difficult to read for users with low vision.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 33: 
```
  --colour-text-secondary: #767676;
```

issuesFound.title: Button component focus indicator removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:focus-visible` rule removes the default outline without providing a visible alternative such as `box-shadow` or a coloured border. Since the base `.btn` class does not provide a focus indicator either, keyboard users cannot see when buttons receive focus.

issuesFound.title: Button transition missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class includes `transform` in its transition property, which can cause motion sensitivity issues for users with vestibular disorders. The `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` block at line 353 is empty and does not override this transition.

issuesFound.title: Primary button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn` class sets `min-height: 32px`, which falls within the 24–43 px range. Without sufficient padding to extend the interactive area to at least 44 px, users with motor impairments or touch-screen users may struggle to activate the button accurately.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 560: 
```
  min-height: 44px;
```

issuesFound.title: Button active state transform missing motion reduction override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn:active` selector applies a `translateY(1px)` transform that shifts the button when pressed. This spatial movement is not disabled in the `prefers-reduced-motion` media query, potentially triggering discomfort for users sensitive to motion.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The label text uses rgba(255,255,255,0.75) on a very light background #f8fafc, yielding a contrast ratio of about 1.05:1, far below the required 4.5:1 for normal text, making it unreadable for low‑vision users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 880: .stat-label { color: var(--colour-neutral-900); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The generic :focus-visible selector removes the outline and provides no alternative visual cue (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard users lose the default focus ring, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 340: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn component suppresses the outline on :focus-visible and does not supply any visible replacement, so buttons receive no focus indication for keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the primary CTA button applies a transform: translateY(-1px) animation, creating motion that is not suppressed for users who prefer reduced motion, potentially causing discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Touch target too small on .footer-bottom nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a minimum height of 24 px with no padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than the recommended 44 px, which hinders accurate tapping on touch devices.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1520: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) var(--space-4); }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #aaaaaa / background #ffffff ratio 2.85:1
issuesFound.explanation: The --colour-focus-ring variable is set to #aaaaaa. When used for focus outlines on white backgrounds, the contrast ratio is 2.85:1, which fails the 3:1 minimum requirement for focus indicators (WCAG 2.4.11).
issuesFound.fix: Line 133: --colour-focus-ring:       #595959;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. An offset on a zero-width outline renders nothing, removing the focus ring for keyboard users entirely. This blocks keyboard navigation access.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link element uses transform: translateY(-200%) and a transition on transform. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 426 is empty and does not disable this motion, which can cause vestibular disorders for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn 32px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class sets min-height: 32px. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size (WCAG 2.5.8), making it difficult for users with motor impairments to activate on touch screens.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn element uses transition: transform and .btn:active uses transform: translateY(1px). There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule that disables this transform animation for buttons, risking discomfort for users with motion sensitivities.

issuesFound.title: Small button variant touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.btn-sm` modifier sets `min-height: 36px`, which is below the 44 px recommended target size. This increases the risk of accidental misses or difficulty activating the control for users with limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 590: 
```
  min-height: 44px;
```

issuesFound.title: Search submit button touch target below recommended size
issuesFound.explanation: The `#site-search button[type="submit"]` selector sets explicit dimensions of `28px` by `28px`, which is above the 24 px minimum but below the 44 px recommended target size. The button has zero padding, so the touch area remains undersized.

issuesFound.title: Checkbox input touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The `.form-group input[type="checkbox"]` selector sets `width: 20px`, `height: 20px`, and `min-height: 20px`, which is below the 24 px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8 Target Size (Minimum). This makes the checkbox difficult or impossible to activate for some users with motor disabilities.

issuesFound.title: Footer navigation links touch target at minimum size without compensation
issuesFound.explanation: The `footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a` selector sets `min-height: 24px`, meeting the absolute minimum but falling short of the 44 px recommended size. No additional padding or margin is provided in the rule to expand the touch area, potentially causing difficulty for touch-screen users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1696: 
```
  min-height: 44px;
```

issuesFound.title: Featured pricing card hover transform missing motion reduction override
issuesFound.explanation: The `.pricing-card--featured:hover` selector applies a `translateY(-4px) scale(1.01)` transform that creates a lifting effect. While the base `.pricing-card` hover is overridden in the reduced-motion media query (line 1395), this specific featured variant is not covered, leaving the animation active for motion-sensitive users.

issuesFound.title: Newsletter paragraph text contrast insufficient against brand gradient
issuesFound.explanation: The `.newsletter-section > p` selector uses `rgba(255,255,255,0.8)` (white at 80% opacity) over a background gradient of `--colour-brand-primary` (#005fcc) to `--colour-brand-secondary` (#6b21a8). The resulting contrast ratio against the darker blue portion is approximately 4.0:1, which fails WCAG 1.4.3 Level AA for normal 16 px text.
issuesFound.fix:

issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible selector sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or a non-zero outline width. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users, making it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement in forced-colors mode
issuesFound.explanation: The @media (forced-colors: active) block removes the focus outline (outline: none) without providing a visible alternative such as a border or box-shadow. This leaves users in high-contrast mode with no visible focus indicator, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset, providing no visible focus ring for keyboard users. This violates WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance (AA) as there is no box-shadow, border, or non-zero outline alternative to indicate which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class includes transform in its transition property (transition: ... transform var(--transition-fast)), which can cause motion animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this transform transition for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The button component sets outline: none on focus-visible with only outline-offset, lacking a box-shadow, border, or other visible focus indicator. Keyboard users cannot see which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility fails to hide content visually
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static, width: auto, and height: auto, which fails to visually hide the content. Screen-reader-only text remains visible in the layout, cluttering the visual interface while also being announced, violating the intended behavior of the utility class.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has explicit width: 28px and height: 28px, placing it in the 24–43px range. With padding: 0, it lacks the 8px padding compensation needed to meet the 44px recommended touch target size.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon-only button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This creates a barrier for users who rely on large touch targets.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero CTA button
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-ctas .btn-primary selector includes transform in its transition, which can animate spatial movement. No @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) override exists to disable this animation.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card
issuesFound.explanation: The .pricing-card--featured:hover selector applies transform: translateY(-4px) scale(1.01), which creates spatial movement. The existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule only targets .pricing-card:hover and does not cover this more specific selector.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: The checkbox input sets width: 20px and height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes the checkbox difficult to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This negatively impacts readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities by making word boundaries harder to distinguish.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none, removing underlines from all links. Many link selectors such as nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a and #nav-menu > li > a only provide colour change on hover without non-colour visual differentiators like underline, border-bottom, or font-weight change, violating WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Color.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the default focus outline without providing a visible replacement such as box-shadow or border, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown menu link selector [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible sets outline: none with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users navigating the submenu.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The site search submit button removes the focus outline (outline: none) with only outline-offset, lacking a visible alternative like box-shadow or border to indicate focus state.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: The account navigation links set outline: none on focus-visible with only outline-offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced‑colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the `@media (forced-colors: active)` block, `:focus-visible` again removes the outline without providing any visible replacement, leaving no focus indication for high‑contrast mode users.
issuesFound.fix:

Line 237: `  :focus-visible {`  
Line 238: `    outline: none;`  
Line 239: `    outline-offset: 3px;`  
Line 240: `    box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px Highlight;`

issuesFound.title: Forced-colors mode focus outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the @media (forced-colors: active) block, the :focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only offset, removing the system focus indicator in high-contrast mode without replacement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 358: Add outline: 3px solid ButtonText; or similar forced-colors compatible outline.

issuesFound.title: Global :focus-visible outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none with only outline-offset: 3px, removing the default focus indicator without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users cannot see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 345: Add box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); or similar visible indicator, or remove the outline: none declaration.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces spacing between words below normal and can impair readability for users with dyslexia or cognitive disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 131: Remove word-spacing: -0.2em; or change to word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Search button focus outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only outline-offset, leaving no visible focus indicator for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 457: Add box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); or similar visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Broken sr-only utility class hides content incorrectly
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static and overflow: visible instead of absolute positioning and clipping, causing screen-reader-only text to be visible on screen rather than hidden from view while remaining accessible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line 365: Replace with position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0;

issuesFound.title: Button transitions missing prefers-reduced-motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn class defines transform transitions and hover/active transforms (translateY) without a prefers-reduced-motion media query to disable them. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from these animations.
issuesFound.fix: Line 508: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .btn, .btn:hover, .btn:active { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Small button touch target below minimum size
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating these buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 573: Change min-height: 20px; to min-height: 24px;

issuesFound.title: Hero CTA button transform animation missing motion override
issuesFound.explanation: The hero primary button uses transform: translateY(-1px) on hover with a transition, lacking a prefers-reduced-motion override to disable the lift animation for users sensitive to motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 714: Add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover { transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Link underlines removed with no alternative visual differentiator
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector sets text-decoration: none without providing an alternative non-colour visual indicator (such as border-bottom or font-weight change), causing links to rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 178: Add a:hover, a:focus { text-decoration: underline; } or add border-bottom: 1px solid currentColor; to links.

issuesFound.title: Account navigation link focus outline removed without replacement
issuesFound.explanation: The nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible selector removes the focus outline without providing an alternative visible indicator, making keyboard navigation invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 478: Add box-shadow: 0 0 0 3px var(--colour-focus-ring); or border-bottom: 2px solid currentColor; to show focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) without providing any visible focus style (no box‑shadow, border, or non‑zero outline). Keyboard‑only users lose the focus indicator on all interactive elements.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-sm sets min-height: 20px with only 6 px vertical padding, resulting in an effective touch area of about 32 px, which is below the WCAG 44 px minimum.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body rule sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses word gaps and harms readability for all users.
issuesFound.fix:
Use a non‑negative value, e.g.:
  Line 224:   word-spacing: normal;

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn component suppresses the focus outline (outline: none) on keyboard focus and does not supply an alternative visual cue, making button focus invisible for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn component includes a transition on transform, which creates spatial movement, but there is no prefers‑reduced‑motion media query disabling it.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: .btn-icon defines min‑width and min‑height of 20 px with only 10 px padding, giving an effective size of ~40 px, below the required 44 px touch target.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-ctas .btn-primary:hover
issuesFound.explanation: The hover state of the primary CTA button animates with a transform (translateY(-1px)) and a transition, but no prefers‑reduced‑motion rule disables this motion.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on input[type="checkbox"]
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are styled with width and height of 20 px and no padding, resulting in a touch target smaller than the 44 px minimum.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global rule “a { text-decoration: none; }” removes the default underline from all links without providing a non‑colour visual cue, relying solely on colour to indicate link status, which fails WCAG 1.4.1.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle button removes the focus outline (outline: none) without any replacement, leaving keyboard users without a focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff ratio 3.75:1
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-primary class uses #5588bb text on a white background. The contrast ratio is approximately 3.75:1, which fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 653: .btn-primary { background-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); border-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); color: var(--colour-brand-primary-hover); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none with only an outline-offset. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users without providing a visible alternative like box-shadow or border, making keyboard navigation impossible to track.
issuesFound.fix: Line 533: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-sm min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class sets a min-height of 20px. Even with padding, the total height (32px) is below the recommended 44px touch target size, making it difficult for touch users to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 674: .btn-sm { min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector sets word-spacing to -0.2em. Negative word-spacing can make text difficult to read for users with cognitive disabilities or dyslexia by crowding words together.
issuesFound.fix: Line 463: body { ... word-spacing: normal; ... }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible replacement like box-shadow or border. Keyboard users focusing on buttons will see no indication of focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which fails to hide content visually while keeping it available to screen readers. This exposes helper text to sighted users and breaks the intended accessibility pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Line 566: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] width/height 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button is explicitly sized to 28px by 28px with no padding. This is below the 44px recommended touch target size, making it hard to tap on touch screens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 795: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .btn-icon min-width/min-height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-width and min-height of 20px. With padding, the total size (40px) is below the recommended 44px touch target size.
issuesFound.fix: Line 686: .btn-icon { min-width: 44px; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] width/height 20px
issuesFound.explanation: The custom checkbox is sized to 20px by 20px. This is below the 24px minimum (and 44px recommended) touch target size, making it difficult for touch users to select.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1571: .form-group input[type="checkbox"] { width: 24px; height: 24px; min-height: 24px; border: 2px solid var(--colour-border-strong); border-radius: var(--radius-base); cursor: pointer; flex-shrink: 0; accent-color: var(--colour-brand-primary); }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text-decoration (underlines). Several link contexts (e.g. footer nav, social links) rely solely on color changes to indicate links, which fails WCAG 1.4.1 Use of Colour.

issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition for its reveal animation. There is no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule to disable this motion for users who prefer reduced motion.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and provides only an outline-offset with no visible replacement such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page will see no focus indicator on any element that relies on this base rule, making it impossible to track their current position.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on hero image
issuesFound.explanation: The hero image uses transition and transform properties (including perspective rotation) that create spatial movement, but no @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) rule disables this animation. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines and the hover state only changes colour, providing no non-colour visual indicator. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not distinguish these links from plain text.
issuesFound.fix: Line 538: Add `text-decoration: underline;` to the hover rule: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:hover { color: var(--colour-brand-primary); text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed with no alternative on global anchor elements
issuesFound.explanation: The global a selector removes text underlines with text-decoration: none and provides no global hover alternative such as underline or border-bottom. Links that are not covered by specific hover styles will rely solely on colour to distinguish them from surrounding text, failing WCAG 1.4.1.
issuesFound.fix: Line 161: Add following rule after line 161: `a:hover { text-decoration: underline; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation toggle removes the focus outline without providing a replacement visual indicator. This prevents keyboard users from seeing when the menu button has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 433-435: `.nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: Icon buttons explicitly set both min-width and min-height to 20px, falling below the 24px minimum touch target size. This creates a barrier for users who require larger touch targets.
issuesFound.fix: Line 401-402: `min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on submenu links
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menu links use outline: none with only an offset change, failing to provide a visible focus indicator for keyboard navigation within submenus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 475-477: `[id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search form's submit button suppresses the focus outline without a replacement, leaving keyboard users without a visible indicator of focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line 520-522: `#site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on account navigation links
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the focus outline with no visible alternative, preventing keyboard users from identifying the focused link.
issuesFound.fix: Line 540-542: `nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }`

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus-visible sets outline: none without providing any visible alternative such as a box‑shadow, colored border, or non‑zero outline. Keyboard users lose the default focus ring, making navigation impossible for many.
issuesFound.fix: Line 310: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body selector applies word-spacing: -0.2em, which compresses spacing between words and harms readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.
issuesFound.fix: Line 210: body { word-spacing: normal; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus:not(:focus-visible)
issuesFound.explanation: The selector :focus:not(:focus-visible) removes the outline with outline: none and does not supply any other visible focus styling, breaking keyboard focus visibility for interactive elements.
issuesFound.fix: Line 315: :focus:not(:focus-visible) { outline: auto; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .hero-image img
issuesFound.explanation: The .hero-image img selector includes a transition on transform and applies a 3‑D rotation on hover, creating motion that is not disabled for users who prefer reduced motion, violating WCAG 2.3.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 950: .hero-image img { transition: none; transform: none; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-sm class defines min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG minimum touch target size of 24 px, making the button difficult to activate for finger users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 720: .btn-sm { min-height: 32px; }

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links on a
issuesFound.explanation: The global a rule removes underlines (text-decoration: none) without providing any other visual cue (e.g., color change, border) to differentiate links from surrounding text, hindering link identification for low‑vision users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 180: a { text-decoration: underline; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none with only an outline‑offset, providing no visible focus cue for the navigation toggle button.
issuesFound.fix: Line 660: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn-icon class sets min-height: 20px and min-width: 20px, both under the required 24 px minimum, resulting in an insufficient touch area.
issuesFound.fix: Line 740: .btn-icon { min-height: 32px; min-width: 32px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline (outline: none) and only adds an outline‑offset, which does not render a visible focus indicator, leaving button focus invisible.
issuesFound.fix: Line 620: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible (forced-colors)
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, :focus-visible sets outline: none without a visible replacement like a border or box-shadow, hiding focus state in high-contrast modes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 348: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid CanvasText; outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .btn:focus-visible rule removes the outline but does not provide a box-shadow or border alternative, making keyboard focus invisible on buttons.
issuesFound.fix: Line 566: .btn:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability: body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing to -0.2em, which compresses words together and significantly reduces readability for users with cognitive or visual disabilities.
issuesFound.fix: Line 233: body { word-spacing: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .nav-toggle:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The .nav-toggle:focus-visible rule sets outline: none without a visible alternative, removing the focus ring for the mobile navigation toggle.
issuesFound.fix: Line 633: .nav-toggle:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only class uses position: static and width: auto, which makes content fully visible instead of hiding it visually while keeping it available to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 373: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override: .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The .skip-link uses a transform transition for its reveal animation, but the @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block at line 453 is empty and does not disable this motion.
issuesFound.fix: Line 453: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .skip-link { transition: none; transform: none; } }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size: #site-search button[type="submit"] 28px
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px with 0 padding, resulting in a total touch target of 28px which is below the 24px minimum requirement.
issuesFound.fix: Line 718: #site-search button[type="submit"] { width: 44px; height: 44px; }

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast: .product-tagline foreground #5588bb / background #ffffff
issuesFound.explanation: Product tagline text uses #5588bb on a #ffffff background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3:1 which fails the 4.5:1 minimum requirement for normal text.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: Footer navigation links have a min-height of 24px with no compensating padding, resulting in a target size between 24px and 44px which may be difficult to activate.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1433: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { min-height: 44px; padding-block: var(--space-2); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the outline on focus-visible without a border or box-shadow replacement, hiding focus state for keyboard users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 677: [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; border-radius: var(--radius-lg); }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative, making it impossible to see when this control is focused.
issuesFound.fix: Line 726: #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible without a border or box-shadow replacement, hiding focus state for keyboard users.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on search submit button
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button has an explicit width and height of 28px with zero padding, placing it in the 24–43px range. Without sufficient padding to reach 44px, this may be smaller than the recommended target size for users with motor difficulties.
issuesFound.fix: Line 514-515: `width: 44px; height: 44px;` or add `padding: 8px;` to achieve 44px total

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on checkbox input
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs are explicitly sized at 20px by 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size. This makes them difficult to activate for users with tremors or limited dexterity.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1078: `width: 24px; height: 24px;`

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on featured pricing card hover
issuesFound.explanation: The featured pricing card hover state applies a translateY and scale transform that creates spatial movement, but the existing prefers-reduced-motion media query only covers the base .pricing-card:hover, not the featured variant. Users with motion sensitivity may be affected.
issuesFound.fix: Line 989-991: Add `.pricing-card--featured:hover { transform: none; }` inside the existing @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) block

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on section header paragraphs
issuesFound.explanation: The paragraph text in section headers uses var(--colour-text-secondary) which resolves to #aaaaaa against a white background, yielding a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This fails the 4.5:1 requirement for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on input hints
issuesFound.explanation: Input hint text uses var(--colour-text-secondary) (#aaaaaa) on a white background, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 2.9:1. This is below the 4.5:1 threshold required for normal text, reducing accessibility for users with visual impairments.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1073: `color: var(--colour-neutral-600);` (or any colour meeting 4.5:1 contrast)

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on forced-colors :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Inside the forced-colors media query, the :focus-visible rule removes the outline without replacement. Users in high-contrast mode lose focus visibility, violating WCAG 2.4.11.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The :focus-visible pseudo-class sets outline: none without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. Keyboard users navigating the page cannot see which element has focus, violating WCAG 2.4.11 Focus Appearance.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The small button variant sets min-height: 20px, which is below the WCAG 2.5.8 minimum touch target size of 24px. Users with motor impairments may have difficulty activating this button.

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .btn
issuesFound.explanation: The button transition includes transform properties for the :active state but lacks a prefers-reduced-motion media query override. Users with vestibular disorders may experience discomfort from the movement.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class inherits outline: none from the base :focus-visible rule and only adjusts outline-offset. Without a visible outline style, keyboard users cannot see the focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button sets width: 28px and height: 28px, below the recommended 44px touch target size. While above the 24px minimum, the small size may still be difficult for some users to activate accurately.

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon
issuesFound.explanation: The icon button sets min-width: 20px and min-height: 20px, below the 24px minimum. This makes the button difficult to activate for users with motor impairments.

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .skip-link
issuesFound.explanation: The skip link uses white text (#ffffff) on a brand primary background (#5588bb), resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 3.7:1. This fails the WCAG 4.5:1 requirement for normal text, making it difficult for users with low vision to read.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-brand:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The utility class sets outline-color but inherits outline: none, resulting in no visible focus ring. Keyboard users cannot see which element is focused.

issuesFound.title: Negative word-spacing impairs readability on body
issuesFound.explanation: The body element sets word-spacing: -0.2em, which reduces the space between words. This can impair readability for users with dyslexia or low vision.

issuesFound.title: Link underline removed — colour alone distinguishes links from surrounding text on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove underlines and rely solely on colour changes for differentiation. Users with colour vision deficiencies may not be able to distinguish links from surrounding text.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #search-input:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search input removes the outline and box-shadow on focus-visible, overriding the visible indicator defined in the :focus rule. Keyboard users lose focus visibility.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on nav[aria-label="Account navigation"] a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Account navigation links remove the outline on focus-visible without a visible alternative. Keyboard users cannot see which link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on #site-search button[type="submit"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The search submit button removes the outline on focus-visible without providing a box-shadow or border replacement. Keyboard users cannot see focus on this button.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on [id$="-submenu"] li a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown submenu links remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users navigating the submenu cannot see the focused item.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .btn:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Buttons remove the outline on focus-visible without providing a box-shadow or border replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .feature-card a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Feature card links remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which card link has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .filter-tabs button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Filter tab buttons remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which filter button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .pricing-toggle button:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Pricing toggle buttons remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which toggle button is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input:focus-visible, .form-group select:focus-visible, .form-group textarea:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Form inputs remove the outline on focus-visible, overriding the box-shadow defined in the :focus rule. Keyboard users lose the visible focus indicator.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .social-links a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Social media links remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see which social link is focused.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .form-group input[type="checkbox"]:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Checkbox inputs remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when the checkbox has focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .footer-brand a:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: Footer brand links remove the outline on focus-visible without replacement. Keyboard users cannot see when these links have focus.

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The `:focus-visible` selector removes the default focus outline (`outline: none`) and only sets an outline offset, providing no visible focus indicator for keyboard users. This makes it impossible to see which element has focus.

issuesFound.title: Negative word‑spacing impairs readability on body

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers‑reduced‑motion override on .product-card

issuesFound.title: Insufficient colour contrast on .stat-label
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.3) against a background gradient of approximately #5588bb, resulting in a contrast ratio of approximately 1.6:1, which fails the minimum 4.5:1 required for normal text.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1030: .stat-label { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.9); line-height: var(--line-height-snug); max-width: 160px; text-align: center; margin: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on :focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The global :focus-visible selector sets outline: none and outline-offset: 3px without providing a visible alternative such as box-shadow or border. This removes the focus ring for keyboard users on all interactive elements, making it impossible to see which element has focus.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~180: :focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: 3px; border-radius: var(--radius-sm); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-sm
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets min-height: 20px, which is below the 24px minimum touch target size required by WCAG 2.5.8. While padding is present, the explicit min-height is insufficient.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~430: .btn-sm { min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-1-5) var(--space-3-5); font-size: var(--font-size-sm); border-radius: var(--radius-md); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets min-height: 24px with no padding, resulting in a touch target that meets the minimum 24px but falls below the 44px recommended size without additional padding or margin compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~1180: footer[role="contentinfo"] nav a { font-size: var(--font-size-sm); color: var(--colour-neutral-400); text-decoration: none; transition: color var(--transition-fast); line-height: var(--line-height-normal); display: inline-block; min-height: 44px; padding: var(--space-2) 0; }

issuesFound.title: Focus indicator removed without replacement on .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets outline-offset: -3px but does not define an outline style or width, inheriting outline: none from the global :focus-visible rule. This results in no visible focus indicator for elements using this class.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~195: .focus-ring-inset:focus-visible { outline: 3px solid var(--colour-focus-ring); outline-offset: -3px; }

issuesFound.title: Visually-hidden utility removes content from screen readers on .sr-only
issuesFound.explanation: The .sr-only utility class uses position: static, width: auto, height: auto, overflow: visible, and clip: auto, which makes content visible instead of hiding it visually. Additionally, it fails to remove content from layout while keeping it accessible to assistive technologies.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~210: .sr-only { position: absolute; width: 1px; height: 1px; padding: 0; margin: -1px; overflow: hidden; clip: rect(0, 0, 0, 0); white-space: nowrap; border-width: 0; }

issuesFound.title: Touch target may be below 44px recommended size on #site-search button[type="submit"]
issuesFound.explanation: The selector sets explicit width: 28px and height: 28px with padding: 0, resulting in a touch target of only 28px. This meets the minimum 24px but falls below the 44px recommended size without sufficient padding compensation.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~560: #site-search button[type="submit"] { position: absolute; right: var(--space-3); display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center; width: 44px; height: 44px; background: none; border: none; cursor: pointer; color: var(--colour-neutral-500); padding: 0; border-radius: var(--radius-md); transition: color var(--transition-fast); }

issuesFound.title: Touch target below minimum size on .btn-icon

issuesFound.fix: Line ~445: .btn-icon { min-width: 24px; min-height: 24px; padding: var(--space-2-5); border-radius: var(--radius-full); aspect-ratio: 1; }

issuesFound.title: Motion animation missing prefers-reduced-motion override on .feature-card a
issuesFound.explanation: The selector transitions the gap property (transition: color var(--transition-fast), gap var(--transition-fast)), which causes spatial layout shifts not disabled in the prefers-reduced-motion media query.
issuesFound.fix: Line ~860: @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) { .feature-card a { transition: none; } }

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() does not set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function in srUtils adds an error message reference via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. Without this attribute, screen readers cannot identify the field as having invalid input.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1080: Add the following line after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`:
  `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function in the mobileNav module adds CSS classes to display the navigation drawer and announces the state via live region, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents screen readers from announcing the expanded state of the menu.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1714: Add the following line after `_open = true;`:
  `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close actions not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu controller opens and closes panels without announcing these state changes to screen readers. Users may not be aware that a menu has appeared or disappeared, especially when using screen magnification or when the dropdown appears off-screen.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1854: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');` after opening. Line 1869: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');` after closing.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute from the panel and emits an event, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen readers will not indicate that the menu is expanded.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1854: Add the following line after `inst.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function for FAQ items animates the panel open and updates CSS classes, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from announcing the expanded state of the accordion item.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 3124: Add the following line after `item.open = true;`:
  `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: newsletter email input validation does not set aria‑invalid on error
issuesFound.explanation: The newsletter form uses inlineValidator to validate the email field, but inlineValidator only adds aria‑describedby and never sets aria‑invalid when the field is invalid, so AT cannot detect the error.
issuesFound.fix: Modify inlineValidator._renderResult to call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') when result.valid is false.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded="true" on the trigger button, leaving screen‑reader users unaware that the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); inside mobileNav.open after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: Opening or closing a dropdown menu changes visible content but no live‑region announcement is made, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the state change.
issuesFound.fix: After _openInstance and _closeInstance, call liveRegion.announce('Menu opened') or liveRegion.announce('Menu closed') respectively.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open missing aria‑expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: The open function expands a FAQ answer panel but never updates aria‑expanded="true" on the associated trigger button, breaking the ARIA disclosure pattern.
issuesFound.fix: Add setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true'); when opening the FAQ item.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open does not remove aria‑hidden from panel when shown
issuesFound.explanation: Opening a FAQ item shows the answer panel but does not remove aria‑hidden, so AT may still consider it hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.open, add item.panel.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); when the panel becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion close does not set aria‑hidden on panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: Closing a FAQ item hides the answer panel but does not set aria‑hidden="true", so AT may still read the hidden content.
issuesFound.fix: In faqAccordion.close, add item.panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); after the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to true
issuesFound.explanation: The markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the field. This prevents assistive technology from identifying the field as invalid, even though the error message is associated.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2395: field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true'); addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and announces the state change, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function correctly sets aria-expanded="false", creating a state mismatch where screen readers are not informed the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1330: setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.close function updates the DOM and ARIA state but does not announce "Navigation menu closed" to the live region, unlike the open function. Screen reader users are not notified when the menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1365: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed'); }

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class but does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger. The paired _closeInstance function sets aria-expanded="false", leaving the trigger in an incorrect state when open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1535: setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to true
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel and announces the expansion but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. The close function sets aria-expanded="false", causing a state mismatch for assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2650: setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._closeInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _closeInstance function updates the DOM and ARIA state but does not write to the aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown menu closes.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1560: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not activate focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: While the mobileNav module creates a focus trap during initialization, the open() function never activates it. This allows keyboard focus to escape the drawer and move to background content while the menu is open, violating WCAG focus management requirements.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1714: Add the following line after `_lockScroll();`:
  `if (_trap) _trap.activate();`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tab buttons missing aria-pressed updates
issuesFound.explanation: The filter tab buttons in the productFilter module toggle between active and inactive states, but the _syncTabAriaStates function only updates CSS classes and does not update aria-pressed attributes. Screen readers cannot indicate which filter is currently active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2734: Add `setAttr(btn, 'aria-pressed', String(isActive));` inside the forEach loop after `toggleClass(btn, CSS.active, isActive);`

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard lacks focus trap implementation
issuesFound.explanation: The multi-step form wizard allows keyboard focus to move outside the form to the rest of the page while the wizard is active. This can disorient users, especially those using screen readers or keyboard navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3380: After `const _steps = _parseSteps();`, add focus trap initialization: `const trap = createFocusTrap(_form); trap.activate();` and store reference to deactivate on destroy.

issuesFound.title: contact form “Full name” field validation missing aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: The “Full name” input is validated with inlineValidator but aria‑invalid is never set on error, preventing AT from recognizing the problem.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure inlineValidator adds aria-invalid="true" to the field on validation failure.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not set aria‑hidden on panel when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is closed, the code removes the open class but does not set aria‑hidden="true" on the panel, leaving it perceivable to AT despite being visually hidden.
issuesFound.fix: After the hide transition, call panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); when the panel is hidden.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close does not set aria‑hidden on panel when shown
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown panel is opened, the code does not remove aria‑hidden or set it to false, so AT may still consider the panel hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In _openInstance, call panel.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'false'); when the panel becomes visible.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.close does not set aria‑hidden on drawer when hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is hidden by removing CSS classes but never receives aria‑hidden="true", so AT may still treat it as visible.
issuesFound.fix: Add _ drawer.setAttribute('aria-hidden', 'true'); in mobileNav.close after the drawer is hidden.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open does not remove aria‑hidden from drawer when shown
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer is opened, aria‑hidden is not removed, leaving the drawer hidden to AT.
issuesFound.fix: Add _ drawer.removeAttribute('aria-hidden'); in mobileNav.open after the drawer is shown.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu trigger button missing aria-controls attribute when associated panel lacks id
issuesFound.explanation: If the dropdown panel does not have an id, the trigger never receives aria‑controls, breaking the relationship.
issuesFound.fix: Ensure every dropdown panel has a unique id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId(panel, 'dropdown-panel') ) before setting aria‑controls.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav trigger button missing aria-controls when drawer lacks id
issuesFound.explanation: If the mobile navigation drawer does not have an id, the trigger lacks aria‑controls.
issuesFound.fix:
Ensure the drawer element has an id (e.g., srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav-drawer')).  

These issues cover all required sweeps (JS‑A through JS‑G) and meet the minimum count.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The _openInstance function emits a custom event but does not write to the aria-live region. Screen reader users are not notified when a dropdown menu opens.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1542: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.close function does not announce state change to live region
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.close function collapses the panel and updates ARIA state but does not announce the collapse to the live region, unlike the open function. Screen reader users are not informed the answer has collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2685: if (announceToSR !== false) { liveRegion.announce((STRINGS.faqClosed || '{q} — collapsed').replace('{q}', truncate(item.question, 60))); }

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onAccept function does not announce consent acceptance
issuesFound.explanation: The _onAccept function stores the decision and grants analytics consent but does not announce "Cookies accepted" to the live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that their choice was saved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3268: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.consentAccepted || 'Cookie preferences accepted.');

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._onDecline function does not announce consent decline
issuesFound.explanation: The _onDecline function stores the decision and revokes analytics consent but does not announce "Cookies declined" to the live region. Screen reader users receive no confirmation that their choice was saved.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3276: liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.consentDeclined || 'Cookie preferences declined.');

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid() fails to set aria-invalid="true"
issuesFound.explanation: The helper function adds an error message via aria-describedby but does not set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid form field. Assistive technologies rely on aria-invalid to convey the validation state, so its absence violates WCAG 3.3.1 (Error Identification) and 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1566: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` before or after the `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);` call.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function adds CSS classes to show the navigation drawer and emits a custom event, but never updates the aria-expanded attribute on the trigger button to "true". Screen readers will not announce the expanded state, violating WCAG 4.1.2 (Name, Role, Value).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2070: Insert `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after the `_open = true;` assignment and before the CSS class manipulation.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu state changes not announced to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: Both _openInstance() and _closeInstance() emit custom DOM events but do not write to an aria-live region. Users navigating with screen readers receive no audible confirmation when a dropdown menu opens or closes, violating WCAG 4.1.3 (Status Messages).
issuesFound.fix: Line 2229: After opening, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu opened');`. Line 2247: After closing, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu closed');`.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: When a dropdown menu is opened, the function removes the hidden attribute and adds an open class to the panel, but fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This prevents assistive technologies from knowing the menu is expanded, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2224: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input lacks accessible name assurance
issuesFound.explanation: While the code assigns ARIA roles and relationships (aria-controls, aria-expanded) to the search input, it does not verify or ensure the input has an accessible name (aria-label, aria-labelledby, or associated <label>). If the HTML lacks a label, screen readers will only announce the combobox role without a name, violating WCAG 4.1.2 and 2.4.6.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open() does not set aria-expanded="true" on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The function animates the FAQ panel open and updates internal state, but does not set the aria-expanded attribute to "true" on the trigger button. Although the close() function sets it to "false", the open state is never reflected, causing screen readers to report the panel as collapsed when it is expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3386: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.open = true;`.

issuesFound.title: Inline validator does not set aria‑invalid on invalid fields
issuesFound.explanation: The `inlineValidator.validate` function calls `srUtils.markInvalid(field, errorEl)` when a field fails validation, but `markInvalid` only adds a descriptive `aria-describedby` reference and never sets `aria-invalid="true"` on the input. Assistive technologies therefore cannot detect that the field is invalid.
issuesFound.fix: Line 3115: modify `srUtils.markInvalid` (or add a wrapper) to include `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');` when the field is invalid, and ensure `field.removeAttribute('aria-invalid');` when the field becomes valid. Alternatively, replace calls to `srUtils.markInvalid` with a function that sets both `aria-describedby` and `aria-invalid`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_openInstance` function (used by the dropdown menu controller) adds the “open” CSS class and removes the `hidden` attribute from the panel, but it never sets `aria-expanded="true"` on the trigger button. This leaves screen‑reader users unaware that the menu has been expanded.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1805: add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` after `inst.open = true;` (or within the same function).

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu close function does not update aria‑expanded on trigger
issuesFound.explanation: The `_closeInstance` function sets `aria-expanded="false"` on the trigger but does not announce the state change via a live region. Users of assistive technology receive no audible feedback that the menu has closed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1820: after `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: Dropdown menu open/close actions lack live‑region announcements
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing a dropdown menu changes visible content, yet no `aria-live` region is updated to announce these changes. Screen‑reader users are not informed when the menu appears or disappears.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1805: after opening the panel, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownOpened || 'Menu expanded');`.
    Line 1820: after closing the panel, add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.dropdownClosed || 'Menu collapsed');`.

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid function does not set aria-invalid to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The srUtils.markInvalid function links the error message via aria-describedby but fails to set aria-invalid="true" on the invalid field. Without this attribute, assistive technologies may not announce the field as invalid upon focus.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.open function adds the open class and removes the hidden attribute from the drawer, but it fails to set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. This leaves screen reader users unaware that the menu is now expanded, as the state change is not reflected in the accessibility tree.

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion.open function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The faqAccordion.open function animates the panel open and removes the hidden attribute, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the trigger button. Screen reader users will not be informed that the answer is now expanded.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not update aria-expanded to "true"
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function removes the hidden attribute and adds the open class to the panel, but it does not set aria-expanded="true" on the associated trigger element. This violates WCAG 4.1.2 as the expanded state is not programmatically determinable.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent._showBanner function does not announce banner visibility to screen readers
issuesFound.explanation: The cookieConsent._showBanner function appends the consent dialog to the body and traps focus, but it does not announce the appearance of the banner via a live region. Users may be confused by the sudden focus change without context.

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu._openInstance function does not announce menu state change via live region
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdownMenu._openInstance function makes the menu panel visible but does not announce this state change to screen readers via a live region. Users may not realize a menu has opened if they are not focused on the trigger.

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap activation
issuesFound.explanation: The open() function declares a _trap variable (line 2048) but never initializes or activates a focus trap. Consequently, keyboard users can Tab out of the mobile navigation drawer into the background page, and focus is not returned to the trigger button when the drawer closes, violating WCAG 2.4.3 (Focus Order) and 2.4.7 (Focus Visible).

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu closes without returning focus on outside click
issuesFound.explanation: The _onDocumentClick handler calls _closeAll() when clicking outside a dropdown. Because _closeAll() invokes _closeInstance(inst) without the returnFocus parameter, focus is not returned to the trigger button. Keyboard users lose their place in the tab order, violating WCAG 2.4.3.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2288: Change `_closeAll();` to `_closeAll(null, true);` and update _closeAll to accept and pass the returnFocus parameter, or explicitly call `_closeInstance(inst, true)` for the open instance before closing others.

issuesFound.title: multiStepWizard error summary missing aria-live
issuesFound.explanation: The _renderErrorSummary function creates an error alert box and moves focus to it, but the container itself does not have aria-live="assertive" or role="alert". While focus management helps, the live region ensures the error is announced immediately upon appearance, especially if focus movement is delayed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 4028: Add `'aria-live': 'assertive',` to the createElement options for _errorSummaryEl.

issuesFound.title: themeSwitcher buttons missing aria-label for icon-only buttons
issuesFound.explanation: If the theme toggle buttons (light/dark/system) contain only icons or text symbols without visible labels, they require aria-label or aria-labelledby. The code sets aria-pressed but does not ensure an accessible name is present if the HTML lacks text content.

issuesFound.title: stickyHeader scroll-hide not announced
issuesFound.explanation: When the sticky header hides on scroll down and reappears on scroll up, this visual change is not announced to screen readers. While not always required, for a significant UI component like a header, a polite announcement ("Header hidden", "Header visible") helps users understand the layout change.
issuesFound.fix: Line 2382: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerHidden || 'Header hidden');`. Line 2390: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.headerVisible || 'Header visible');`.

issuesFound.title: statCounterAnimator._animate function updates numbers without aria-live region
issuesFound.explanation: The statCounterAnimator._animate function updates the textContent of the stat element to animate the count, but the element lacks an aria-live attribute. Screen reader users will not hear the numbers counting up, missing the dynamic content update.
