issuesFound.title: srUtils.markInvalid missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: When marking a field as invalid, the function adds `aria-describedby` but fails to set `aria-invalid="true"`. Screen readers cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `addDescribedBy(field, errorEl);`: Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation drawer is toggled by the `_trigger` button, but the code never updates `aria-expanded` on this trigger. Screen readers cannot announce whether the menu is expanded or collapsed, violating WCAG 4.1.2 Name, Role, Value.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `open()`: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `if (_open || !_trigger || !_drawer) return;`
  Line in `close()`: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!_open) return;`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch input missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: The search input (`_inputEl`) acts as a combobox that shows/hides the suggestion listbox, but `aria-expanded` is never toggled on the input. Screen readers cannot announce when the listbox is visible.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `_open()`: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `if (_searchState.open) return;`
  Line in `_close()`: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!_searchState.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu toggle missing aria-expanded updates
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown trigger buttons (`inst.trigger`) open and close panels but do not have their `aria-expanded` attribute updated to reflect the state change. Assistive technologies cannot convey the expanded/collapsed state to users.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `_openInstance()`: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `inst.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`
  Line in `_closeInstance()`: Add `setAttr(inst.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');` immediately after `if (!inst.open) return;`

issuesFound.title: pricing setPeriod missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the billing period is switched, prices update visually, but screen readers do not announce the change or the new pricing mode (monthly/annual).
issuesFound.fix: Line after `_syncToggleState(period);`: Add `liveRegion.announce(period === 'year' ? STRINGS.pricingAnnual : STRINGS.pricingMonthly);`

issuesFound.title: pricing period buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The billing period buttons (`[data-period]`) act as a toggle group (monthly vs annual) but only toggle CSS classes. They lack `aria-pressed` to indicate the active/pressed state to assistive technologies.

issuesFound.title: Form validation errors not reflected in aria‑invalid
issuesFound.explanation: inlineValidator adds error messages but never sets aria‑invalid="true" on invalid fields, violating WCAG 4.1.2.
issuesFound.fix: In inlineValidator.validate when result.valid is false call field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'true') and on success remove the attribute.

issuesFound.title: Toggle function does not update aria‑expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The mobileNav.toggle function opens and closes the mobile navigation drawer but never sets aria‑expanded on the hamburger button trigger, leaving screen‑reader users unaware of the drawer’s state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (and the open/close helpers) add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', String(!_open)) so the button’s aria‑expanded reflects the drawer’s visibility.

issuesFound.title: Dynamic content update not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: pricing.setPeriod updates plan prices and discount badges but does not write to any aria‑live region, so users of assistive tech are not informed of price changes.
issuesFound.fix: After updating each price, call liveRegion.announce(`Price changed to ${formattedPrice}`) or update a dedicated status element with aria‑live="polite".

issuesFound.title: aria‑expanded not initialised on page load
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav trigger button lacks an initial aria‑expanded attribute, so its default state is ambiguous to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.init after selecting _trigger add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false').

issuesFound.title: User action result not announced via live region
issuesFound.explanation: productFilter.applyFilters updates the product count badge and empty‑state message but does not announce the new count to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: After updating the count, set _statusEl.textContent with aria‑live="polite" or call liveRegion.announce(`Showing ${count} products`).

issuesFound.title: Toggle button does not update aria‑pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile‑nav hamburger button acts as a two‑state toggle (open/closed) but never updates aria‑pressed, so AT cannot convey its state.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.toggle (and open/close) add setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-pressed', String(!_open)).

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open function missing aria-expanded update
issuesFound.explanation: When an FAQ item is opened, the trigger button (`item.trigger`) does not receive `aria-expanded="true"`. Although the `close()` function sets it to false, the opening action omits setting it to true, leaving the state incorrect for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line in `open()`: Add `setAttr(item.trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'true');` immediately after `item.panel.removeAttribute('hidden');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Opening and closing the mobile navigation drawer changes the visible content significantly, but no announcement is made to screen readers to indicate the state change.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `open()` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:open', { drawer: _drawer });`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navOpen || 'Navigation menu opened');`
  Line in `close()` after `emit(_trigger, 'mobilenav:close', { drawer: _drawer });`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.navClosed || 'Navigation menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: Dropdown menus open and close without announcing their state change to screen readers, leaving users unaware that content has appeared or disappeared.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `_openInstance()` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:open', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu opened');`
  Line in `_closeInstance()` after `emit(inst.trigger, 'dropdown:close', { trigger: inst.trigger, panel: inst.panel });`: Add `liveRegion.announce('Dropdown menu closed');`

issuesFound.title: faqAccordion open/close missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: FAQ items expand and collapse without notifying screen readers, so users may not realize the content has changed or which item is now active.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `open()` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:open', { item });`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqOpened ? STRINGS.faqOpened(item.question) : 'Answer expanded');`
  Line in `close()` after `emit(_containerEl, 'faq:close', { item });`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.faqClosed ? STRINGS.faqClosed(item.question) : 'Answer collapsed');`

issuesFound.title: scrollToTop missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When the scroll-to-top button is activated, the page scrolls to the top and focus moves, but no confirmation is announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `setTimeout(() => { returnEl.focus({ preventScroll: true }); }, 300);`: Add `liveRegion.announce(STRINGS.scrolledToTop || 'Scrolled back to top of page');`

issuesFound.title: srUtils.markValid missing aria-invalid clearance
issuesFound.explanation: When clearing the invalid state, the function removes the error description but does not clear `aria-invalid`, leaving the field marked as invalid for screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line at end of function (before closing brace): Add `field.setAttribute('aria-invalid', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: On page load, the mobile navigation trigger button does not have `aria-expanded` initialized to "false", so screen readers do not know the initial collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `_initialised = true;`: Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav init missing aria-controls
issuesFound.explanation: The trigger button (`_trigger`) does not have `aria-controls` pointing to the drawer (`_drawer`), so screen readers cannot establish the relationship between the button and the controlled region.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `_initialised = true;` (and after ensuring _drawer exists): Add `setAttr(_trigger, 'aria-controls', srUtils.ensureId(_drawer, 'mobile-nav'));`

issuesFound.title: dropdownMenu register missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: When registering a new dropdown, the trigger button does not have `aria-expanded` initialized to "false", violating the requirement that the initial state be programmatically determined.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `const inst = { trigger, panel, open: false, hoverTimer: null };`: Add `setAttr(trigger, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: siteSearch init missing aria-expanded initialization
issuesFound.explanation: The search input is initialized as a combobox but lacks `aria-expanded="false"` to indicate the initial collapsed state of the suggestion list.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `const listbox = suggestionListRenderer.mount(_inputEl);`: Add `setAttr(_inputEl, 'aria-expanded', 'false');`

issuesFound.title: mobileNav missing focus trap
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile navigation drawer opens, keyboard focus is not trapped within the drawer. Users can tab out of the menu into the background content, violating WCAG 2.4.3 Focus Order and 2.4.7 Focus Visibility for modal dialogs.

issuesFound.title: cookieConsent actions missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When users accept or decline cookies, the banner disappears but no confirmation is announced to screen readers, leaving users uncertain if their action was successful.
issuesFound.fix:
Line in `_onAccept()` before `_hideBanner();`: Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookies accepted');`
  Line in `_onDecline()` before `_hideBanner();`: Add `liveRegion.announce('Cookies declined');`

issuesFound.title: productFilter tabs missing aria-selected

issuesFound.title: activeFilterChips render missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When filter chips are added or removed, the visual list updates but screen readers do not announce the change in active filters.
issuesFound.fix: Line at end of `render()` function (before closing brace): Add `liveRegion.announce('Active filters updated');`

issuesFound.title: discountCodeInput apply missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a discount code is applied and prices update, no announcement is made to screen readers about the successful application or price change.
issuesFound.fix: Line after `_applyDiscount(result.discountPct);`: Add `liveRegion.announce('Discount applied and prices updated');`

issuesFound.title: planComparisonHighlighter highlightColumn missing live region announcement
issuesFound.explanation: When a plan column is highlighted (on hover or programmatically), the visual change is not announced to screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line at end of `highlightColumn()` function: Add `liveRegion.announce('Plan highlighted');`

issuesFound.title: Dynamically hidden element not removed from accessibility tree
issuesFound.explanation: When the mobile‑nav drawer is closed, it is hidden only by removing the is‑open CSS class; the element remains display:block and is still in the accessibility tree without aria‑hidden.
issuesFound.fix: In mobileNav.close add setAttr(_drawer, 'aria-hidden', 'true') and in open remove that attribute.

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The TextInput component receives an invalid prop to style error states but does not map it to aria-invalid. Screen-reader users cannot perceive when the input contains invalid data.
issuesFound.fix: Line 840: Add aria-invalid to the input element: `<input id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-input', invalid ? 'form-input--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses aria-controls and aria-haspopup but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the open/closed state to assistive technology. Screen-reader users cannot tell whether the submenu is currently expanded or collapsed.
issuesFound.fix: Line 940: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the button element: `<button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-haspopup="true" aria-expanded={isOpen} className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>`

issuesFound.title: Integrations marquee clone not hidden from assistive technology
issuesFound.explanation: The duplicated list of integration logos used for the infinite scroll animation is not marked with aria-hidden="true", causing screen-reader users to hear the list of integrations twice.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1540: Add aria-hidden="true" to the cloned list: `<ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">`

issuesFound.title: Filter tab buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The filter buttons behave as toggle buttons that select a category but do not communicate their selected state to assistive technology. They need aria-pressed to indicate which filter is active.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1750: Add aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}: `<button type="button" className={['filter-tab-btn', activeFilter === tab.value ? 'filter-tab-btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} tabIndex={activeFilter === tab.value ? 0 : -1} onClick={() => onChange(tab.value)} aria-pressed={activeFilter === tab.value}>`

issuesFound.title: Billing toggle buttons missing aria-pressed state
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly/annual billing toggle buttons select a billing period but lack aria-pressed to communicate which option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix:
Line 1684: Add aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}: `<button type="button" id="billing-monthly" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')} aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'}>Monthly</button>`
  Fix: Line 1694: Add aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}: `<button type="button" id="billing-annual" className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')} aria-pressed={value === 'annual'}>Annual{' '}<Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>`

issuesFound.title: Mobile navigation section disclosure buttons missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The buttons that expand/collapse submenu sections in the mobile navigation have aria-controls but lack aria-expanded to indicate whether their associated panel is currently open.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1270: Add aria-expanded={isExpanded} to the button: `<button type="button" aria-controls={sectionId} aria-expanded={isExpanded} className="mobile-nav__link mobile-nav__link--trigger" onClick={() => setOpenSectionId(isExpanded ? null : item.href)}>`

issuesFound.title: Newsletter email input references non-existent error element
issuesFound.explanation: The email input has aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error" when in error state, but no element with that ID is rendered because FormGroup is not passed the errorId prop. This creates a broken ARIA reference.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1496: Pass errorId and errorMessage props to FormGroup: `<FormGroup label="Email address" htmlFor="newsletter-email" errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined} required>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The <nav> element containing skip links lacks an aria-label, making it indistinguishable from other navigation landmarks when multiple <nav> elements are present on the page.
issuesFound.fix: Line 596: Add aria-label to the nav: `<nav aria-label="Skip links">`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid attribute

issuesFound.fix: Line 855: Add aria-invalid to the textarea: `<textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />`

issuesFound.title: Hero section landmark missing accessible name

issuesFound.fix: Line 1410: Add aria-labelledby referencing the heading: `<section className="hero-section" aria-labelledby="hero-heading">`

issuesFound.title: Desktop navigation active link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: The active navigation link uses a visual class to indicate the current page but lacks aria-current="page", so screen-reader users cannot identify which page they are currently on.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1090: Add aria-current="page" conditional on item.current: `<a href={item.href} className={['nav-link', item.current ? 'nav-link--current' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} aria-current={item.current ? 'page' : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: TextInput component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The `TextInput` component accepts an `invalid` prop but does not map it to `aria-invalid`, so screen readers cannot announce the invalid state of form fields.
issuesFound.fix: Line 672: `<input ... aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown disclosure button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button uses `aria-controls` and `aria-haspopup` but lacks `aria-expanded` to communicate the open/closed state of the submenu to assistive technology users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1203: `<button ... aria-expanded={isOpen} ...>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `IconButton` component wraps its children in `aria-hidden="true"` and requires an `aria-label` prop, but none is provided. This results in a button with no accessible name for screen reader users.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1783: `<IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" ...>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "All tools" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle button shows visual active state via CSS class `filter-tab-btn--active` but lacks `aria-pressed` to communicate the selected state to assistive technology.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: `<button ... aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'all'} ...>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Scanning" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle button shows visual active state via CSS class `filter-tab-btn--active` but lacks `aria-pressed` to communicate the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: `<button ... aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'scanning'} ...>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Design" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle button shows visual active state via CSS class `filter-tab-btn--active` but lacks `aria-pressed` to communicate the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: `<button ... aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'design'} ...>`

issuesFound.title: FilterTabs "Testing" button missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The filter toggle button shows visual active state via CSS class `filter-tab-btn--active` but lacks `aria-pressed` to communicate the selected state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1490: `<button ... aria-pressed={activeFilter === 'testing'} ...>`

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel next button missing accessible name

issuesFound.fix: Line 1788: `<IconButton aria-label="Next testimonial" ...>`

issuesFound.title: MobileNav section trigger missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The button that expands/collapses mobile navigation subsections has `aria-controls` but no `aria-expanded` attribute, preventing screen readers from announcing the expanded/collapsed state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1254: `<button ... aria-expanded={isExpanded} ...>`

issuesFound.title: NewsletterForm email input broken aria-describedby reference
issuesFound.explanation: The email input references `aria-describedby="newsletter-email-error"`, but no element with this id exists in the DOM because `FormGroup` was not passed the `errorId` prop. This creates a broken reference that confuses screen readers.
issuesFound.fix: Line 1786: `<FormGroup ... errorId="newsletter-email-error" errorMessage={email.state.touched ? email.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation landmark missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The `<nav>` element wrapping the skip links lacks an `aria-label` or `aria-labelledby` attribute. Since the page contains multiple navigation landmarks (main navigation, footer navigation, etc.), this generic nav region cannot be distinguished by screen reader users when navigating by landmarks.
issuesFound.fix: Line 581: `<nav aria-label="Skip navigation">`

issuesFound.fix: Line 1895: `<FormGroup ... errorId="contact-message-error" errorMessage={message.state.touched ? message.state.error : undefined}>`

issuesFound.title: Textarea component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Textarea component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states, but does not map this to aria-invalid on the underlying textarea element. Assistive technology cannot convey the invalid state to users.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid attribute to the textarea: <textarea id={id} aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} className={['form-textarea', invalid ? 'form-textarea--error' : '', className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: NavDropdown trigger button missing aria-expanded
issuesFound.explanation: The dropdown trigger button controls a visible sub-menu using the isOpen boolean state, but lacks aria-expanded to communicate the expanded/collapsed state to screen reader users. This prevents assistive technology from announcing whether the submenu is currently visible.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-expanded={isOpen} to the trigger button: <button id={triggerId} ref={triggerRef} type="button" aria-controls={menuId} aria-expanded={isOpen} aria-haspopup="true" className="nav-link nav-link--dropdown-trigger" onClick={onToggle}>

issuesFound.title: IntegrationsBar cloned list missing aria-hidden
issuesFound.explanation: The second list of integration logos is a DOM duplicate used only for visual marquee looping, but lacks aria-hidden="true". Screen reader users will encounter duplicate content, creating redundant verbosity and confusion.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-hidden to the cloned list: <ul role="list" className="integrations-bar__list" aria-hidden="true">

issuesFound.title: BillingToggle buttons missing aria-pressed
issuesFound.explanation: The monthly and annual billing toggle buttons indicate the selected billing period using the value state, but lack aria-pressed. Assistive technology cannot convey which billing option is currently selected.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-pressed to monthly button: <button type="button" id="billing-monthly" aria-pressed={value === 'monthly'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'monthly' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('monthly')}>Monthly</button>; Line: Add aria-pressed to annual button: <button type="button" id="billing-annual" aria-pressed={value === 'annual'} className={['billing-toggle__btn', value === 'annual' ? 'billing-toggle__btn--active' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} onClick={() => onChange('annual')}>Annual{' '}<Badge variant="success">Save 20%</Badge></button>

issuesFound.title: SkipLinks navigation missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The SkipLinks component renders a nav element without an aria-label. Since there are multiple nav elements on the page (main navigation, footer navigation), this landmark lacks a unique accessible name to distinguish it from other navigation regions.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label to the nav: <nav aria-label="Skip navigation">

issuesFound.title: Checkbox component missing aria-invalid mapping
issuesFound.explanation: The Checkbox component accepts an invalid prop to indicate error states, but does not map this to aria-invalid on the underlying checkbox input. Screen reader users cannot detect when the checkbox has invalid input.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-invalid attribute to the input: <input id={id} type="checkbox" aria-invalid={invalid ? 'true' : undefined} aria-describedby={errorId} className="checkbox-input" {...rest} />

issuesFound.title: TestimonialsCarousel previous button missing accessible name
issuesFound.explanation: The carousel previous slide button is an icon-only button (containing only the "‹" character) but lacks an aria-label attribute. The IconButton component requires an aria-label prop, but it is not provided, leaving screen reader users without any indication of the button's purpose.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-label="Previous testimonial" to the IconButton: <IconButton aria-label="Previous testimonial" aria-controls={trackId} onClick={handlePrev} className="testimonials-track__prev">‹</IconButton>

issuesFound.title: DesktopNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Navigation links indicating the current page (item.current === true) receive a visual style modifier but lack aria-current="page". Screen reader users cannot identify which page is the current page in the navigation.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current to the current link: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current
issuesFound.explanation: Mobile navigation links indicating the current page (item.current === true) receive a visual style modifier but lack aria-current="page". Assistive technology cannot identify the current page location.
issuesFound.fix: Line: Add aria-current to the current link: {item.label}

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name

issuesFound.explanation: The visual ring span inside the `Spinner` component is decorative but lacks `aria-hidden="true"`. The text label is provided via `VisuallyHidden`, so the decorative graphic should be hidden.
issuesFound.fix: Line 583: <span className="spinner__ring" aria-hidden="true" />

issuesFound.title: Reusable Button component missing aria-busy during loading
issuesFound.explanation: The `Button` component accepts an `isLoading` prop and renders a spinner, but does not apply `aria-busy="true"` to the button element. Assistive technology users are not informed that the button is in a busy state.
issuesFound.fix: Line 505: <button type={type} disabled={isDisabled} aria-disabled={isDisabled || undefined} aria-busy={isLoading || undefined} className={['btn', `btn--${variant}`, `btn--${size}`, className].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} {...rest} >

issuesFound.title: MobileNav current link missing aria-current

issuesFound.title: PlanCard article missing accessible name

{stat.value}

issuesFound.title: Modal dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The dialog element lacks aria-modal="true", which is necessary to indicate to assistive technology that the content outside the dialog is inert while the dialog is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <div ref={dialogRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-labelledby={titleId} aria-describedby={description ? descId : undefined} className={['modal-dialog', `modal-dialog--${size}`].join(' ')} onClick={(ev) => ev.stopPropagation()}>

issuesFound.title: MobileNav dialog missing aria-modal attribute
issuesFound.explanation: The mobile navigation panel is a modal dialog but does not declare aria-modal="true", preventing screen readers from recognizing that the background content is disabled while the menu is open.
issuesFound.fix: Line: <div id={menuId} ref={panelRef} role="dialog" aria-modal="true" aria-label={`${SITE.name} navigation`} className={['mobile-nav', isOpen ? 'mobile-nav--open' : ''].filter(Boolean).join(' ')} hidden={!isOpen}>

issuesFound.title: Modal aria-modal incorrectly placed on overlay
issuesFound.explanation: The aria-modal attribute is incorrectly placed on the backdrop overlay instead of the dialog element itself, which may cause assistive technology to incorrectly identify the modal boundary.
